content: Have you ever passed a secret note to a friend and didn’t want anyone else to read it? Online messages work the same way! To keep messages private, people use something called encryption. Think of encryption like a magic code that changes your message into a jumbled puzzle that only your friend can solve. When you send a message, it gets locked inside a secret treasure chest. Only the person with the special key—the magic password—can open the chest and read the message inside. Without the key, the message looks like a bunch of confusing letters and numbers, so no one else can understand it. This keeps your secrets safe when you chat online with friends or family. Encryption is like having a superpower that protects your messages from being peeked at by strangers. That’s how the internet helps keep your private talks just between you and the people you trust!
